symptoms.character: high fever with violent shaking chills and drenching sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic febrile response indicates a complicated, potentially bacteraemic prostatic infection rather than simple cystitis.
Rigors and fever from a urinary source are a recognised marker of systemic infection and sepsis risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe dysuria with frequency and urgency
symptoms.character: burning pain on voiding, desperate frequency, tiny volumes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Irritative lower-urinary-tract symptoms accompany prostatic inflammation.
Also causes dysuria and frequency; non-specific on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Obstructive voiding — hesitancy, slow weak stream, tiny volumes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prostatic oedema narrows the prostatic urethra causing obstructive symptoms and threatened retention.
Progressive obstruction warns of impending complete retention needing catheterisation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep perineal pain radiating to suprapubic region and lower back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perineal/pelvic pain localising to the prostate, worse on sitting, is characteristic.
Radiation to the back overlaps, but the pain is not colicky unilateral flank pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful ejaculation the night before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painful ejaculation is a fairly specific pointer to prostatic involvement.
Suggests pyuria/bacteriuria but is a non-specific patient observation.
Tachycardia with fever is part of the systemic inflammatory response; patient-obtainable via wearable, admissible but unverified in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisitely tender, warm, boggy, swollen prostate on digital rectal examination

investigation_details.expected_result: Warm, boggy, swollen, exquisitely tender prostate gland
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The confirmatory prostatic finding is unobtainable remotely; the AI must route to in-person examination rather than infer it.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A boggy, exquisitely tender prostate on DRE is the defining in-person finding — sealed clinician measurement, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dense pyuria and bacteriuria on point-of-care urinalysis

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urinalysis and midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_details.expected_result: Dense pyuria and bacteriuria; culture to confirm organism and sensitivities
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without urine testing the organism and sensitivities are unknown; empirical antibiotics require in-person collection and a prescriber.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pyuria/bacteriuria confirm urinary infection; sealed clinician-obtained result, not available via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic tenderness with a non-distended bladder on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bladder not yet distended at examination indicates retention is threatened rather than established; abdominal palpation cannot be done remotely.
Absence of CVA tenderness makes pyelonephritis less likely, but it can only be elicited in person, so pyelonephritis cannot be excluded remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms prostatic involvement; must be performed face-to-face — the AI arranges it rather than performing or inferring it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urinalysis with midstream urine microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms infection and guides antibiotic choice; requires in-person specimen collection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods and blood cultures (FBC, CRP, U&E/creatinine, lactate)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Gauge sepsis severity and renal function in a febrile, tachycardic patient; unobtainable via telehealth.
